Container.Name: TREK
Container.Overview: TREK is a self-hosted, real-time collaborative travel planner with interactive maps, budgets, bookings, packing lists, file management, and more. Plan trips together with your group — changes sync instantly across all connected users. Includes OIDC/SSO support, dark mode, PWA, and a modular addon system (Vacay, Atlas, Collab, Budget, Packing).

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web interface

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Database and app data

Config.Name: Uploads
Config.Description: Uploaded files (photos, documents)

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: At-rest encryption key for stored secrets (API keys, MFA, SMTP, OIDC). Recommended: generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. If unset, falls back to data/.jwt_secret (existing installs) or auto-generates a key (fresh installs).

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: Internal server port (must match the container port mapping above).

Config.Name: NODE_ENV
Config.Description: Node environment (production / development).

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for logs, reminders and scheduled tasks (e.g. Europe/Berlin).

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Log verbosity: info = concise user actions, debug = verbose admin-level details.

Config.Name: TREK_WIKI_DIR
Config.Description: Where the in-app Help pages (/help) read their content from. Leave empty: the container ships the wiki at /app/wiki and finds it automatically. Only set this to serve your own docs from a mounted path. If the path does not exist, Help falls back to fetching the public GitHub wiki (needs outbound network, and tracks the latest release rather than your version).

Config.Name: DEFAULT_LANGUAGE
Config.Description: Default language shown on the login page for users with no saved preference. Browser/OS language is auto-detected first; this is the fallback when no match is found. Supported: de, en, es, fr, hu, nl, br, cs, pl, ru, zh, zh-TW, it, ar.

Config.Name: ALLOWED_ORIGINS
Config.Description: Comma-separated origins allowed for CORS and used as base URL in email notification links (e.g. https://trek.example.com).

Config.Name: APP_URL
Config.Description: Public base URL of this instance (e.g. https://trek.example.com). Required when OIDC is enabled — must match the redirect URI registered with your IdP. Also used as base URL for email notification links.

Config.Name: FORCE_HTTPS
Config.Description: Optional. When true: HTTPS redirect, HSTS header, CSP upgrade-insecure-requests, and secure cookies. Only useful behind a TLS-terminating proxy. Requires TRUST_PROXY.

Config.Name: HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS
Config.Description: When true: adds includeSubDomains to the HSTS header, extending HTTPS enforcement to all subdomains. Only effective when HSTS is active (FORCE_HTTPS=true or NODE_ENV=production). Leave false if you run other services on sibling subdomains over plain HTTP.

Config.Name: COOKIE_SECURE
Config.Description: Auto-derived (true in production or when FORCE_HTTPS=true). Set to false to force session cookies over plain HTTP. Not recommended for production.

Config.Name: TRUST_PROXY
Config.Description: Trusted proxy hops for X-Forwarded-For/X-Forwarded-Proto. Defaults to 1 in production; off in development unless set. Required for FORCE_HTTPS.

Config.Name: ALLOW_INTERNAL_NETWORK
Config.Description: Allow outbound requests to private/RFC-1918 IP addresses. Set to true if Immich or other integrated services are hosted on your local network.

Config.Name: SESSION_DURATION
Config.Description: How long a login session stays valid when 'Remember me' is unchecked (the default): trek_session JWT exp + a browser-session cookie cleared when the browser closes. Accepts 1h, 12h, 7d, 30d, 90d. Defaults to 24h.

Config.Name: SESSION_DURATION_REMEMBER
Config.Description: Session length when 'Remember me' is ticked at login: a longer-lived JWT + persistent cookie that survives browser restarts. Same format as SESSION_DURATION. Defaults to 30d.

Config.Name: ADMIN_EMAIL
Config.Description: Email for the first admin account created on initial boot. Has no effect once any user exists.

Config.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the first admin account created on initial boot. If omitted a random password is generated and printed to the server log. Has no effect once any user exists.

Config.Name: OIDC_ISSUER
Config.Description: OpenID Connect provider URL (e.g. https://auth.example.com).

Config.Name: OIDC_CLIENT_ID
Config.Description: OIDC client ID registered with your identity provider.

Config.Name: OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
Config.Description: OIDC client secret registered with your identity provider.

Config.Name: OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME
Config.Description: Label shown on the SSO login button.

Config.Name: OIDC_ONLY
Config.Description: Set to true to force SSO-only mode. Disables password login and password registration — overrides the granular toggles in Admin > Settings and cannot be changed at runtime. First SSO login becomes admin.

Config.Name: OIDC_ADMIN_CLAIM
Config.Description: OIDC claim used to identify admin users (e.g. groups).

Config.Name: OIDC_ADMIN_VALUE
Config.Description: Value of the OIDC claim that grants admin role (e.g. app-trek-admins).

Config.Name: OIDC_SCOPE
Config.Description: Space-separated OIDC scopes to request. Fully overrides the default — always include openid email profile plus any extra scopes you need (e.g. add groups when using OIDC_ADMIN_CLAIM).

Config.Name: OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL
Config.Description: Override the auto-constructed OIDC discovery endpoint. Useful for providers with a non-standard path (e.g. Authentik).

Config.Name: DEMO_MODE
Config.Description: Enable demo mode (resets all data hourly). Not intended for regular use.

Config.Name: MCP_RATE_LIMIT
Config.Description: Max MCP API requests per user per minute.

Config.Name: MCP_MAX_SESSION_PER_USER
Config.Description: Max concurrent MCP sessions per user.

Config.Name: UNSPLASH_ACCESS_KEY
Config.Description: Optional Unsplash Access Key for trip-cover and place-image search. Without one, TREK uses Unsplash's unauthenticated endpoint, which some datacenter/VPS IPs are blocked from. Get a free key at unsplash.com/developers. Can also be set per-admin in Admin > Settings; this env var overrides that.
